title: Collection of Environmental Samples for Screening Influenza A Viruses
author: Bhumika Prajapati

steps.title: Equip hands with sterile rubber gloves...
steps.instruction: Equip hands with sterile rubber gloves and conceal face with a fresh N95 mask to avoid accidental exposure or cross contamination during the sampling process. (Note: Sterilize gloves with 70-80% ethanolbefore initiating the sample collection)

steps.title: Select the sampling locations within wetlands...
steps.instruction: Select the sampling locations within wetlands lake based on high bird activity to maximize the likelihood of detecting AIV shed into surface water.

steps.title: Collect a minimum of 500 mL...
steps.instruction: Collect a minimum of 500 mL of water sample from the wetland waterbody surface/poultry water in a sterile 1 L jerry can. (Note: Sterilise gloves post sample collection with 70-80% ethanol).

steps.title: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof...
steps.instruction: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof permanent markers directly on the sample container (tube/bottle) including essential details: ○ Sample type ○ Location/site name ○ Date of collection

steps.title: Post-collection, store the collected water samples...
steps.instruction: Post-collection, store the collected water samples in a potable refrigerator (4ºC) to maintain the cold chain, essential for sample preservation.

steps.title: Discard used gloves and mask in...
steps.instruction: Discard used gloves and mask in a biohazard bag immediately after sample collection.

steps.title: Use a fresh pair of sterile...
steps.instruction: Use a fresh pair of sterile gloves and a mask for subsequent sample collection sites.

steps.title: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A
steps.instruction: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A.

steps.title: Try to collect the fresh fecal material if possible
steps.instruction: Try to collect the fresh fecal material if possible

steps.title: Collect approximately 2-3 g of faecal...
steps.instruction: Collect approximately 2-3 g of faecal matter in 3 mL of VTM using sterile autoclaved wooden spoons. (Note: Quantity of faecal matter may be augmented relative to the volume of sampling vial being used)

steps.title: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof...
steps.instruction: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof permanent markers directly on the sample container (tube/bottle), including essential details: ○ Sample type ○ Location/site name ○ Date of collection

steps.title: Immediately store the collected sample VTMs...
steps.instruction: Immediately store the collected sample VTMs in an ice box maintained at 4 ºC until further sample processing.

steps.title: Discard the used gloves, mask and...
steps.instruction: Discard the used gloves, mask and wooden spoons in biohazard bags post sample collection.

steps.title: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A
steps.instruction: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A.

steps.title: Select the sampling locations within wetlands...
steps.instruction: Select the sampling locations within wetlands based on high bird activity to maximize the likelihood of detecting AIV from molted feathers in wetlands.

steps.title: Collect 3 to 5 feathers and...
steps.instruction: Collect 3 to 5 feathers and store in 3 mL VTM from a single sampling site, preferably molted from different bird species in the wetland setups. (Note: Make sure that the quill or calamus is dipped in the VTM)

steps.title: The same sampling strategy is to...
steps.instruction: The same sampling strategy is to be followed for feather collection from poultry fowls

steps.title: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof...
steps.instruction: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof permanent markers directly on thesample container (tube/bottle) including essential details: ○ Sample type ○ Location/site name

steps.title: Date of collection Immediately store the...
steps.instruction: Date of collection Immediately store the collected VTMs containing feather samples in an ice boxmaintained at 4 ºC until further sample processing

steps.title: Discard the used gloves and mask in biohazard bags
steps.instruction: Discard the used gloves and mask in biohazard bags.

steps.title: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A
steps.instruction: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A.

steps.title: Select the sampling locations within wetlands...
steps.instruction: Select the sampling locations within wetlands lake based on high bird activity to maximize the likelihood of detecting AIV shed into surface water.

steps.title: Collect sediment soil samples from banks...
steps.instruction: Collect sediment soil samples from banks of wetland water bodies in sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes. (Note: Try collecting soil samples from different locations at the same sampling site to cover the maximum area)

steps.title: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof...
steps.instruction: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof permanent markers directly on the sample container (tube/bottle) including essential details: ○ Sample type ○ Location/site name ○ Date of collection

steps.title: Store the collected sediment samples at...
steps.instruction: Store the collected sediment samples at 4 ºC until further pre-processing.

steps.title: Discard the used gloves and mask in biohazard bags
steps.instruction: Discard the used gloves and mask in biohazard bags.

steps.title: Place the air sampler at a...
steps.instruction: Place the air sampler at a minimum height of 1 meter (use a tripod stand if available or hold the air sampler manually at a constant height and location) above ground level and within 1–2-meter distance ranges from the collection site.

steps.title: Place a sterile gelatin filter (Pore size
steps.instruction: Place a sterile gelatin filter (Pore size: 3 microns, Diameter: 80 mm) using the provided fitting apparatus for the air sampler using sterile forcep making sure that thefilter does not break or crack during the installation. (Note: Make sure that the gelatin filter is handled and installed while wearing sterile gloves)

steps.title: Switch on the air sampler for...
steps.instruction: Switch on the air sampler for 20 minutes with preset collection parameters (50 L/minutes), to collect a total air volume of 1000 L.

steps.title: Remove the gelatin filter from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the gelatin filter from the air sampler using clean forcep and collect it in 6 mL of VTM. Gentlyshake the VTM vial to dissolve the collected gelatin filter.

steps.title: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof...
steps.instruction: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof permanent markers directly on the sample container (tube/bottle) including essential details: ○ Sample type ○ Location/site name ○ Date of collection

steps.title: Store the VTM vial at 4 ºC until further processing
steps.instruction: Store the VTM vial at 4 ºC until further processing.

steps.title: Repeat the same method at different...
steps.instruction: Repeat the same method at different locations, making sure that the air sampler is sterilised with 70-80% ethanol for each new sampling location.

steps.title: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A
steps.instruction: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A.

steps.title: Use the prepared sterile electrostatic dust...
steps.instruction: Use the prepared sterile electrostatic dust cloth pieces to collect swabs from surfaces of poultry farms. (Note: Make sure to cover maximum area around the poultry environment)

steps.title: Use different EDC swabs for different...
steps.instruction: Use different EDC swabs for different locations or depending on the number of cages present in a particular poultry farm.

steps.title: Submerge the collected swab in sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes
steps.instruction: Submerge the collected swab in sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof...
steps.instruction: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof permanent markers directly on the sample container (tube/bottle) including essential details: ○ Sample type ○ Location/site name Date of collection

steps.title: Store the collected swab at 4 ºC until further pre-processing
steps.instruction: Store the collected swab at 4 ºC until further pre-processing.

steps.title: Discard the used gloves and mask in biohazard bags
steps.instruction: Discard the used gloves and mask in biohazard bags.

steps.title: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A
steps.instruction: Follow personal safety precautions as mentioned earlier in section A.

steps.title: Collect approximately 2-3 g of poultry...
steps.instruction: Collect approximately 2-3 g of poultry litter in 3 mL of VTM using

steps.title: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof...
steps.instruction: Label collected samples clearly using waterproof permanent markers directly on the sample container (tube/bottle) including essential details: ○ Sample type ○ Location/site name

steps.title: Immediately store the sample VTMs in...
steps.instruction: Immediately store the sample VTMs in an ice box maintained at 4 ºC

steps.title: Discard the used gloves, mask and...
steps.instruction: Discard the used gloves, mask and wooden spoons in biohazard bags post sample collection.
